overview.short: Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park, shortly known as MMRC and Unity Park, is a garden, museum, recreation park and research centre for culture of different ethnic a
overview.description: Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park, shortly known as MMRC and Unity Park, is a garden, museum, recreation park and research centre for culture of different ethnic and religious groups of Manipur, with a special focus on the Meitei culture. It is located in Khangabok town in Thoubal district of Manipur. It houses a temple of Pakhangba of traditional Meitei religion (Sanamahism), a Hindu temple, a Buddhist temple, a church and a mosque, promoting communal harmony.

topPlaces.name: Thoubal
topPlaces.description: Thoubal is a town and municipal council with 18 wards, serving as the district headquarters in Thoubal district, in the Indian state of Manipur. The name 'Thoubal' derives from 'Athouba,' meaning brave people, symbolizing the courageous residents of the district.

topPlaces.name: Khangabok
topPlaces.description: Khangabok is a village located about 25 km south of Imphal, in the state of Manipur, India. Its jurisdiction falls under the Thoubal District Division.

topPlaces.name: Wangjing, Manipur
topPlaces.description: Wangjing (Meitei pronunciation: /waŋ.jiŋ/) is a town and a municipal council in Thoubal district in the Indian state of Manipur. It is about 6 km from district headquarters and an agricultural town in Thoubal district in the Indian state of Manipur.

topPlaces.name: Kairembikhok, Manipur
topPlaces.description: Kairembikhok is a village in Thoubal District in Manipur, India.

topPlaces.name: Tentha
topPlaces.description: Tentha is a village in Thoubal District in Manipur, India.

topPlaces.name: Kaina (Manipur)
topPlaces.description: Kaina is a small hillock located in the Indian state Manipur, that is sacred to Hindus. It lies on the Imphal – Yariripok road, about 29 kilometres (18 mi) from the state capital, Imphal.

topPlaces.name: Sacred Jackfruit Tree
topPlaces.description: The Sacred Jackfruit Tree is a historical site in the Indian state of Manipur where a jackfruit tree (Artocarpus heterophyllus) growing on the small hill of Kaina was used to carve images of Hindu god Krishna. Rajarshi Bhagya Chandra, earlier known as Shree Jai Singh Maharaja, the King of Manipur in the 18th century, had a dream in which he received instructions from Krishna to carve His images from this tree.

topPlaces.name: Yairipok
topPlaces.description: Yairipok is a town in Thoubal district in the Indian state of Manipur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Imphal Airport (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (146 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park?
faq.a: Stays in Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Menjor Multipurpose Research Centre and Unity Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thoubal, Khangabok, Wangjing, Manipur, Kairembikhok, Manipur, Tentha.
